fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 63 uses strict inequalities (b <= s or a >= e), which treats touching boundaries (e.g., b == s or a == e) as non-overlapping. The tests expect touching boundaries to be considered conflicts, so the condition should use strict inequalities in the opposite direction: the non-overlap condition should be b < s or a > e.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) for s, e in timeline) == False or any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline) == False

constraints.intent: When the new window touches an existing slot boundary (b==s or a==e), the strict-inequality non-overlap check (b<=s or a>=e) incorrectly classifies it as non-overlapping; the expr is FALSE when touching boundaries exist and the bug is present.

spec.expr: all(not (b__1 == s) for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Before using b__1 in the overlap check, if b__1 equals any existing slot start s, the overlap check should detect a conflict; this is violated when touching is allowed.

spec.expr: all(not (b__1 == s or a__1 == e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After result__1 is assigned, no existing slot in the original timeline should touch the new window boundary; if it does, the booking should have been rejected earlier.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] == True and any(b == s or a == e for s, e in timeline for a, b in [window]))

constraints.intent: The function should never return success=True when the new window's boundary touches an existing slot.
